creator: george@m5p.com
text: If it is ZFS, then the only exotic factor on my system is an external USB one-terabyte drive (WDC WD10EZEX-08WN4A0), formatted with GPT and one ZFS partition, that seems to take a variable amount of time to come on line at power up.  I theorized at one point that tasting that drive at an unpredictable time was a factor in the crash.  Your mileage may vary.
